title: Fix soft constraint relaxation dropping compatible constraints
body:
## Summary

This patch fixes the soft constraint relaxation algorithm in `VlRandomizer::next()`.
The previous linear front-to-back scan drops all soft constraints before the first
conflicting one, even when some are compatible with the kept set. The new algorithm
incrementally adds soft constraints from highest to lowest priority, keeping only
those that remain satisfiable, preserving the maximum compatible set.

Discovered while running core-v-verif UVM testbench on Verilator
(https://github.com/openhwgroup/core-v-verif/pull/2724).

## Reproducer

```systemverilog
class C;
  rand bit [7:0] a, b;
  constraint c_hard { a < 200; b < 200; }
  constraint c_s0 { soft b > 100; }   // compatible with c_s2
  constraint c_s1 { soft a == 30; }   // conflicts with c_s2
  constraint c_s2 { soft a == 80; }   // highest priority
endclass

module t;
  initial begin
    C obj = new;
    repeat (20) begin
      if (!obj.randomize()) $stop;
      if (obj.a != 80) $stop;       // c_s2 should win
      if (obj.b <= 100) $stop;      // c_s0 should be preserved
    end
    $write("*-* All Finished *-*\n");
    $finish;
  end
endmodule
```

On master, `b` is unconstrained (values like 7, 34, 37) because the old algorithm
drops `c_s0` along with `c_s1`:

```
%Error: t_randomize_soft_relaxation.v:42:  got=37 exp=101-199
```

## Changes

- `include/verilated_random.cpp` (`VlRandomizer::next`): Replace linear phase loop
  with back-to-front incremental algorithm using SMT push/pop to test each soft
  constraint individually
- `include/verilated_random.cpp`: Add `VlRandomizer::checkSat()` helper that reads
  sat/unsat without parsing variable values
- `include/verilated_random.h`: Declare `checkSat()`

## Test

- Added `test_regress/t/t_randomize_soft_relaxation.{v,py}` -- Verifies that
  compatible lower-priority soft constraints are preserved when a mid-priority
  constraint conflicts with the highest-priority one
- Updated `test_regress/t/t_constraint_unsat.out` -- The new push/pop order
  produces more precise unsat-core reports (fewer spurious constraints listed)
- Updated `test_regress/t/t_constraint_unsat_protect_ids.out` -- Same reason

---
Developed by PlanV GmbH, assisted with Claude Code.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
